concepts.name: Graceful Degradation
concepts.description: Ensuring a system maintains core functionality with predictable performance reduction when components fail or operate under stress.

concepts.name: Silent Data Corruption
concepts.description: Undetected errors during computation or data transfer that propagate silently through system layers and compromise model accuracy.

concepts.name: Defense in Depth
concepts.description: An architectural strategy that enforces robustness across every stage of the machine learning pipeline rather than relying on a single mitigation.

concepts.name: Distribution Shift
concepts.description: The natural evolution of real-world conditions that causes deployment data to differ from training data, degrading model performance.

concepts.name: Covariate Shift
concepts.description: A type of distribution shift where the input data distribution changes while the relationship between inputs and outputs remains constant.

concepts.name: Concept Drift
concepts.description: A type of distribution shift where the underlying relationship between inputs and outputs changes over time.

concepts.name: Label Shift
concepts.description: A type of distribution shift where the distribution of output classes changes without altering the input-output relationship.

concepts.name: Spurious Correlations
concepts.description: Features that are statistically predictive in the training dataset but lack a causal relationship with the target label.

concepts.name: Group Distributionally Robust Optimization
concepts.description: A training technique that minimizes the loss of the worst-case group to force the model to learn features that generalize across contexts.

concepts.name: Maximum Mean Discrepancy
concepts.description: A statistical distance metric used to quantify distribution shift by measuring differences between training and deployment data distributions.

concepts.name: Population Stability Index
concepts.description: A metric that quantifies distribution shift by computing the symmetric difference in log-odds across discretized feature bins.

concepts.name: Kullback-Leibler Divergence
concepts.description: An asymmetric measure of how much information is lost when using a current distribution to approximate a baseline probability density function.

concepts.name: Jensen-Shannon Divergence
concepts.description: A symmetric, bounded variant of Kullback-Leibler divergence used for practical drift detection thresholds.

concepts.name: Kolmogorov-Smirnov Test
concepts.description: A statistical hypothesis test that compares empirical cumulative distribution functions to detect significant distributional differences.

concepts.name: Chi-Square Test
concepts.description: A statistical test for categorical features that compares observed frequencies to expected frequencies under a baseline distribution.

concepts.name: Bonferroni Correction
concepts.description: A multiple testing correction method that divides the significance level by the number of tests to prevent excessive false alarms during monitoring.

concepts.name: Online Learning
concepts.description: A continuous adaptation strategy allowing models to update on new data streams to mitigate concept drift.

concepts.name: Model Ensembles
concepts.description: Maintaining multiple models specialized for different conditions and dynamically selecting or weighting them to adapt to environmental shifts.

concepts.name: Federated Learning
concepts.description: A distributed training approach that enables model adaptation across multiple edge environments while preserving data privacy.

concepts.name: Adversarial Attacks
concepts.description: Deliberate attempts to manipulate model behavior and cause misclassification by adding imperceptible perturbations to inputs.

concepts.name: Adversarial Training
concepts.description: A defense strategy that improves model robustness by augmenting the training dataset with adversarial examples.

concepts.name: Certified Robustness
concepts.description: Mathematical guarantees ensuring a model's prediction will not change within a specified radius of input perturbation.

concepts.name: Data Poisoning
concepts.description: Deliberate corruption of the training process by injecting malicious samples to subvert learned representations and decision boundaries.

concepts.name: Clean-Label Poisoning
concepts.description: An attack that compromises models by injecting malicious samples into datasets without altering their labels to embed a hidden backdoor.

concepts.name: Backdoor Attacks
concepts.description: A type of data poisoning that embeds a hidden trigger causing the model to misclassify specific inputs while otherwise behaving normally.

concepts.name: Spectral Signatures
concepts.description: A statistical defense technique that detects poisoned training data by analyzing the covariance matrix of learned representations for anomalies.

concepts.name: Influence Functions
concepts.description: A technique that approximates the effect of removing a single training data point on the model's loss for a specific test prediction.

concepts.name: Robust Optimization
concepts.description: Modifying the training objective with specialized loss functions to minimize the impact of outliers or poisoned data instances.

concepts.name: Self-Supervised Learning
concepts.description: A learning paradigm that discovers representations by solving pretext tasks, building inherent resilience against distribution shifts.

concepts.name: Contrastive Learning
concepts.description: A self-supervised learning technique that encourages representations to capture invariant features across different views of the same data.
